data.content:
### Install Git via Homebrew (Mac)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/developer-setup.md

Installs Git, a version control system, using Homebrew on macOS. After installation, further configuration for SSH keys and username is recommended for GitHub integration.

```shell
brew install git
```

--------------------------------

### Install Homebrew Package Manager (Mac)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/developer-setup.md

Installs the Homebrew package manager on macOS. Homebrew simplifies the installation of software on macOS. This command downloads and executes the installation script from the official Homebrew repository.

```shell
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js via NVM

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/developer-setup.md

Installs a specific version of Node.js using NVM. The example installs version 20, but it's recommended to install the latest version. NVM allows easy switching between Node.js versions.

```shell
nvm install 20
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Agent Without Configuration File (Node.js)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

The `NEW_RELIC_NO_CONFIG_FILE` environment variable is no longer required to run the agent without a configuration file. Environment variables will now override configuration file settings if both are used.

```bash
# Previously needed:
# export NEW_RELIC_NO_CONFIG_FILE=true

# Now, simply run without the variable or provide other env vars directly.
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node Version Manager (NVM)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/developer-setup.md

Installs Node Version Manager (NVM) to manage multiple Node.js versions. This script downloads and executes the NVM installation script. Always check the NVM GitHub repository for the latest version.

```shell
curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Node.js: Configure New Relic Agent via Server-Side Settings

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This snippet demonstrates how to disable server-side configuration for the New Relic Node.js agent. By default, server-side settings override local configuration. Setting `ignore_server_configuration` to `true` in the configuration file or setting the environment variable `NEW_RELIC_IGNORE_SERVER_CONFIGURATION` to 'true' prevents this override.

```javascript
// In newrelic.js configuration file:
exports.config = {
  // ... other config options
  ignore_server_configuration: true
};

// Or using environment variable:
// NEW_RELIC_IGNORE_SERVER_CONFIGURATION=true node app.js
```

--------------------------------

### Install npm Version 2

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/Migration Guide.md

Command to install npm version 2 globally. This is required for older Node.js versions if you need to upgrade npm separately. Ensure you have Node.js and npm installed.

```shell
$ npm install --global npm@2
```

--------------------------------

### Copy New Relic Configuration File

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/README.md

Copies the stock New Relic configuration file ('newrelic.js') from the installed package to your application's base folder. This file will be customized with your New Relic account details.

```bash
cp ./node_modules/newrelic/newrelic.js ./
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Datastore Instance Reporting (Node.js)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This snippet shows how to configure datastore instance reporting in the New Relic Node.js agent using environment variables. These variables allow enabling or disabling the reporting of database server and database name for operations, which is then displayed in transaction traces and slow query traces. This configuration is applied before the agent initializes.

```bash
# Enable datastore instance reporting for MySQL and MongoDB
export NEW_RELIC_DATASTORE_INSTANCE_REPORTING_ENABLED=true

# Enable reporting of database names
export NEW_RELIC_DATASTORE_DATABASE_NAME_REPORTING_ENABLED=true

# Start your Node.js application
```

--------------------------------

### Node.js Agent Configuration for Environment Variables

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This snippet illustrates how to configure the New Relic Node.js agent using environment variables. It specifically addresses the configuration of the APM apdex value, noting that it should be a float, not a string.

```bash
# Example of setting apdex via environment variable
export NEW_RELIC_APDEX="0.75"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Custom New Relic Config File Name

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This snippet demonstrates how to specify a custom configuration file name for the New Relic agent using the NEW_RELIC_CONFIG_FILENAME environment variable. This allows for flexibility in naming the configuration file beyond the default 'newrelic.js'.

```bash
export NEW_RELIC_CONFIG_FILENAME=my_custom_config.js
```

--------------------------------

### Disable Server-Side Configuration in Node.js Agent

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This snippet demonstrates how to disable server-side configuration for the New Relic Node.js agent. It shows both the local configuration setting (`ignore_server_configuration`) and the corresponding environment variable (`NEW_RELIC_IGNORE_SERVER_SIDE_CONFIG`). This is useful when you want to manage all agent settings locally.

```javascript
// Using local configuration setting:
const newrelic = require('newrelic');
newrelic.configure({ ignore_server_configuration: true });

// Using environment variable:
// NEW_RELIC_IGNORE_SERVER_SIDE_CONFIG=true node app.js
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Prisma Instrumentation in Node.js

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This configuration enables instrumentation for Prisma client versions 4.0.0 and above, capturing detailed query spans, database metrics, and SQL traces. It also facilitates service map connections between the application and the database server. Ensure the minimum supported version of `@prisma/client` is installed.

```javascript
// No specific code example provided for Prisma instrumentation setup, but configuration involves enabling it within the New Relic agent settings.
```

--------------------------------

### Install PostgreSQL via Homebrew (Mac)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/developer-setup.md

Installs PostgreSQL, a relational database system, using Homebrew. This is noted as a requirement for certain tests that depend on the 'pg-native' C library.

```shell
brew install postgresql
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose MySQL Service Configuration (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/docker/Readme.md

Defines a MySQL service for Docker Compose, suitable for integration testing. It specifies the image, port mappings, environment variables for passwordless access, and health check commands to ensure the database is ready. Volume mounting is used to inject custom MySQL configuration.

```yaml
  mysql:
    container_name: nr_node_mysql
    platform: ${DOCKER_PLATFORM:-linux/amd64}
    image: mysql:8.3
    volumes:
      - "./docker/mysql:/etc/mysql/conf.d"
    ports:
      - "3306:3306"
    environment:
      MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD: 1
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysql" ,"-h", "mysql", "-P", "3306", "-u", "root", "-e", "SELECT 1"]
      interval: 1s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 30
```

--------------------------------

### PowerShell Installation Script

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/cloud-tooling/azure-site-extension/Content/README.md

The install.ps1 script is a PowerShell script responsible for the core installation logic of the New Relic Node Agent Site Extension. It handles moving and installing the agent bundle to the correct directory within the Azure host environment.

```powershell
param($installPath, $toolsPath, $siteName, $siteMode)

# Script to install the New Relic Node Agent
Write-Host "Installing New Relic Node Agent..."

# Define paths
$extensionDir = "$installPath\NewRelic.Azure.Websites.Extension.NodeAgent"
$agentDestPath = "C:\home\site\wwwroot\node_modules"
$logFile = "$extensionDir\install.log"

# Ensure destination directory exists
if (-not (Test-Path $agentDestPath)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $agentDestPath | Out-Null
}

# Copy agent files (assuming agent is in a subdirectory within the extension)
# This is a placeholder, actual copy logic would be here.
# Example: Copy-Item -Path "$extensionDir\agent\*" -Destination $agentDestPath -Recurse

# Log installation status
"Installation complete on $(Get-Date)" | Out-File -FilePath $logFile -Append

Write-Host "New Relic Node Agent installation script finished."
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Core Instrumentation - Node.js

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/module-instrumentations.md

Defines the configuration for instrumenting core Node.js modules. It specifies the module to instrument, the type of instrumentation, and the file containing the instrumentation logic. This configuration is managed within the CORE_INSTRUMENTATION object in the shimmer module.

```javascript
var CORE_INSTRUMENTATION = {
  child_process: {
    type: MODULE_TYPE.GENERIC,
    file: 'child_process.js'
  },
  [module_to_instrument]: {
    type: MODULE_TYPE.[type],
    file: 'instrumentation-module-file'
  }, etc...
}

child_process: {
  /* ... */
},

{
  type: MODULE_TYPE.GENERIC,
  file: 'child_process.js'
}

```

--------------------------------

### Configure New Relic Proxy Support

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

Configures the New Relic agent to connect to New Relic via an HTTP or HTTPS proxy. Supports basic proxy authentication and custom certificates for TLS negotiation. This is configured within the newrelic.js file.

```javascript
{
  proxy : {
    host : 'proxy.example.com',
    port : 8080,
    user : 'proxy_user',
    password : 'proxy_password'
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure New Relic Agent (newrelic.js)

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/README.md

Configures the New Relic agent by setting the application name and license key in the 'newrelic.js' file. This step is crucial for connecting your application to your New Relic account.

```javascript
/* File: newrelic.js */
'use strict'
/**
 * New Relic agent configuration.
 *
 * See lib/config/default.js in the agent distribution for a more complete
 * description of configuration variables and their potential values.
 */
exports.config = {
  app_name: ['Your application or service name'],
  license_key: 'your new relic license key',
  /* ... rest of configuration .. */
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure macOS Application Firewall for Node.js

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/developer-setup.md

Runs a script to configure the macOS application firewall to allow incoming connections for the 'node' binary. This is often necessary when running integration tests that involve network communication, preventing repeated authorization prompts.

```shell
sudo ./bin/macos-firewall.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Agent Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/README.md

Installs all the necessary project dependencies for the New Relic Node.js agent using npm. This command should be run after cloning the repository.

```sh
$ npm install
```

--------------------------------

### Enable AI Monitoring Configuration

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This configuration snippet shows how to enable AI Monitoring (AIM) for OpenAI, Amazon Bedrock, and Langchain. It requires setting specific configuration parameters or environment variables. Note that high-security mode will disable AIM.

```json
{
  "ai_monitoring": {
    "enabled": true
  }
}
```

```bash
NEW_RELIC_AI_MONITORING_ENABLED=true
```

--------------------------------

### Batch Script Wrapper for Installation

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/cloud-tooling/azure-site-extension/Content/README.md

The install.cmd file serves as a simple batch script wrapper. Its primary function is to execute the Powershell install.ps1 script, streamlining the installation process when the site extension is applied.

```batch
@echo off

REM Batch script to wrap the Powershell installation script

SET INSTALL_PATH=%~dp0..
SET TOOLS_PATH=%~dp0..	oolswin-x64
ode
ode_modules
SET SITE_NAME=%WEBSITE_SITE_NAME%
SET SITE_MODE=%APP_SETTING_WEBSITE_MODE%

REM Call the Powershell script
powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%~dp0install.ps1" "%INSTALL_PATH%" "%TOOLS_PATH%" "%SITE_NAME%" "%SITE_MODE%"
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenTelemetry Packages for Node.js

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/documentation/nextjs/faqs/cloud-providers.md

This command installs the essential OpenTelemetry packages required for Node.js auto-instrumentation, metric export, and trace export.

```bash
npm install @opentelemetry/api @opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node @opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-proto @opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-proto @opentelemetry/sdk-metrics @opentelemetry/sdk-node @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node
```

--------------------------------

### Batch Script for Uninstallation

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/cloud-tooling/azure-site-extension/Content/README.md

The uninstall.cmd file is a batch script designed to remove the New Relic Node installation artifacts from the Azure environment. It provides a mechanism to clean up the files and configurations added by the site extension.

```batch
@echo off

REM Batch script to remove New Relic Node installation artifacts

SET EXTENSION_DIR=C:\home\SiteExtensions\NewRelic.Azure.Websites.Extension.NodeAgent
SET AGENT_NODE_MODULES_PATH=C:\home\site\wwwroot\node_modules\newrelic
SET LOG_FILE=%EXTENSION_DIR%\install.log

ECHO Uninstalling New Relic Node Agent...

REM Remove node_modules if it exists
IF EXIST "%AGENT_NODE_MODULES_PATH%" (
    RMDIR /S /Q "%AGENT_NODE_MODULES_PATH%"
    ECHO Removed %AGENT_NODE_MODULES_PATH%
)

REM Clean up log file
IF EXIST "%LOG_FILE%" (
    DEL "%LOG_FILE%"
    ECHO Removed %LOG_FILE%
)

REM Remove extension directory if empty (optional, usually handled by Azure)
REM IF EXIST "%EXTENSION_DIR%" (
REM     RMDIR /S /Q "%EXTENSION_DIR%"
REM )

ECHO New Relic Node Agent uninstallation script finished.
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Custom ESM Instrumentation - Node.js

Source: https://github.com/newrelic/node-newrelic/blob/main/NEWS.md

This snippet demonstrates how to configure custom instrumentation entry points for ES Modules (ESM) in Node.js applications. It involves setting a configuration value to specify the entry point for custom instrumentation, allowing for deeper integration with ESM.

```javascript
// Example of setting the custom instrumentation entry point
const newrelic = require('newrelic');

newrelic.esm.custom_instrumentation_entrypoint = './path/to/your/custom/instrumentation.js';

// All newrelic.instrument* methods will work except newrelic.instrumentLoadedModule.
```
